ocr_mistral.text:
Adjutant General's Office
Washington, D.C.
October 7th, 1867

Sir:

I have the honor to acknowledge the receipt from your Office of application for Pension No. 152744, and to return it herewith, with such information as is furnished by the files of this Office.

It appears from the Rolls on file in this Office, that Aaron Chambers was enrolled on the 19th day of Sept., 1864, at Owensboro, Ky., in Co. "G", 118th Regiment of U.S. Cold. Infty Volunteers, to serve three years, or during the war, and mustered into service as a Private on the 19th day of Sept., 1864, at Owensboro, Ky., in Co. "G", 118th Regiment of U.S. Cold. Infty Volunteers, to serve three years, or during the war. On the Muster October Roll of Co. "G" of that Regiment, dated Whites Ranche, Tx., Feb. 6th, 1866, he is reported "Hired of disease in field Hospital Co. Nov. 6/64."

I am, Sir, very respectfully,
Your obedient servant,

The Commissioner of Pensions
Washington, D.C.

Assistant Adjutant General
In charge of Bureau of Pensions

Memoranda
Name of applicant
Address

MB 12

Adjutant General's Office  
Washington, D.C.  
<hw>October 7th, 1867</hw>  
  
Sir:  
  
I have the honor to acknowledge the receipt from your Office  
of application for Pension No. <hw>152744</hw>, and to return it  
herewith, with such information as is furnished by the files of this  
Office.  
  
It appears from the Rolls on file in this Office, that  
<hw>Aaron Chambers</hw> was enrolled on the <hw>19th</hw> day of <hw>Sept.</hw>, <hw>1864</hw>, at <hw>Owensboro</hw>, <hw>Ky.</hw>, in Co. "G", 118th Regiment of U.S. Cold. Infty Volunteers, to  
serve three years, or during the war, and mustered into  
service as a Private on the <hw>19th</hw> day of <hw>Sept.</hw>, <hw>1864</hw>, at <hw>Owensboro</hw>, <hw>Ky.</hw>, in Co. "G", 118th  
Regiment of U.S. Cold. Infty Volunteers, to serve three  
years, or during the war. On the Muster <hw>Roll</hw> of Co. "G" of that Regiment, <hw>dated Whites Ranche, Tx., Feb. 6th, 1866</hw>, he is reported <hw>Died of disease in field Hospital Co. Nov. 6/64</hw>.  
  
I am, Sir, very respectfully,  
Your obedient servant,  
<hw>Geo. E. Spencer</hw>  
  
The Commissioner of Pensions  
Washington, D.C.  
  
Assistant Adjutant General  
<hw>Wm. Johns</hw>  
In charge of Bureau of Pensions

transcribe_handwriting.text:
Adjutant General’s Office,
Washington, D. C.,
October 7th, 1867.

Sir:
I have the honor to acknowledge the receipt from your Office
of application for Pension No. 153,794 and to return it
herewith, with such information as is furnished by the files of this
Office.

It appears from the Rolls on file in this Office, that
Isaac Lahman was enrolled on the 19th
day of Sept. 1861, at Augusta, Indiana in Co. K,
119th Regiment of Indiana Volunteers, to
serve three years, or during the war, and mustered into
service as a Private on the 19th day of Sept
1861, at Indianapolis, in Co. K,
Regiment of Indiana Volunteers, to serve three
years, or during the war. On the Muster Roll of Co. K,
of that Regiment, for the months of Jan & Feb, [18]64,
he is reported Died of disease in field Hospital
near Huntsville Ala.

I am, Sir, very respectfully,
Your obedient servant,
Wm. Teggard
Assistant Adjutant General.

The Commissioner of Pensions,
Washington D. C.

Memoranda
Name of applicant
Address
M. D. B.
